definition:
1. to bloom, abound, to be luxuriant , of fruit-trees, (Odyssey by Homer), etc.; often in participle perfect τεθηλώς, epic feminine τεθαλυῖα, as adjective, luxuriant, exuberant , (Odyssey by Homer); with accusative cognate, οὐ δένδρε᾽ ἔθαλλεν χῶρος the place grew  no trees, θαλλούσης βίον ἐλαίας (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
2. of other natural objects, τεθαλυῖα ἐέρση the fresh  or copious  dew, (Odyssey by Homer); τεθαλυῖα ἀλοιφῆι rich  with fat, (Iliad by Homer); εἰλαπίνηι τεθαλυίηι at a sumptuous  feast, (Iliad by Homer) 
3. of men, to bloom, flourish , (Hesiod), etc. 
4. in bad sense, to be active , νόσος ἀεὶ τέθηλε (Sophocles Tragicus); πήματα ἀεὶ θάλλοντα (Sophocles Tragicus) (ML)
